title: Phaedo Decision & Risk Signal
description: Per-signal shape for the Decision and Risk layer's signals[] (Phaedo Protocol v0.1 §4.2.7). Resolves open question 13 for this layer first — the hero layer for consultation — so a deterministic resolver can consume it without guessing. Other layers' signal shapes remain implementation-defined in v0.1. This is the shape the reference extraction pipeline emits (extraction/fingerprint-update.js) and the consultation resolver consumes (mcp/consult-core.js).

field.description: Producer field id (alias of dimension; retained for back-compat).

dimension.description: Which decision axis this signal captures, e.g. evidence_threshold | speed_vs_quality | options_vs_recommendation | ambiguity_posture | reversible_vs_irreversible.

polarity.description: Per §4.3. A negative (anti-preference) signal takes precedence over a contradicting positive in the same layer.

value.description: The signal value — an enum token (e.g. more_cautious), a scalar in [0,1], or a list.

confidence.description: Per-signal confidence. A resolver SHOULD weigh signals by this and MAY gate on a threshold.

evidence_basis.description: How the signal was learned: observed (from behavior), corroborated (observed + self-reported agree), or self_reported (authored/questionnaire). Consumers SHOULD trust observed/corroborated above self_reported.

source.description: Producer-internal provenance string evidence_basis is derived from.

domain.description: OPTIONAL. The domain this signal pertains to (e.g. financial, legal, writing), in the same case-insensitive-substring namespace as action_descriptor.domain and authored policies. PROTOTYPE emission: the reference extraction tags Decision&Risk signals with a session's inferred domain only when one domain clearly dominates (extraction/domain-inference.js); ambiguous sessions stay untagged. Consumers prefer a matching-domain signal, fall back to untagged, and never borrow a wrong-domain signal. Tag accuracy is under real-use evaluation.

threshold.description: OPTIONAL. A magnitude/numeric gate (e.g. { magnitude_min, amount_gt }). RESERVED — not yet emitted; future work alongside domain scoping.
